summary: I was born and lived in Mexico City for 40 years. Most of my work family also came from there, and despite its pollution and traffic, it shaped who I am. Leaving Mexico City was hard, but I wanted a better life for my children. I often miss my sister and my mom who still live there.

Mexico is my home country where I was born and raised. The situation there became very harsh with insecurity, a deficient education system, and low salaries, which motivated me to move. Despite these challenges, I believe people in Mexico are honest and hardworking. My roots there remain important to me even after moving.

After my husband passed away, I decided to move to the United States with my children for better opportunities and security. We have been living here for 15 months, adapting to the language and cold winters, especially in Anchorage, Alaska. Visiting the U.S. for the first time was a wonderful experience, including trips to Disneyland and SeaWorld. Living here has fulfilled a childhood dream of mine.

I currently live in Anchorage, Alaska with my children and my new husband Chris. It has been challenging adjusting to the long, cold winters and the language, but it has also brought us closer as a family. Anchorage offers nature and security that I couldn't find in Mexico City. This is where I met my husband through my uncle, and where we are building a new life together.

Our best family experience was the first time we visited the United States, where we went to Disneyland. It was a joyful trip for my children and me, filled with fun and new memories. This visit was part of a larger trip that also included SeaWorld and Legoland, making it very special for us.

During our first visit to the United States, we went to SeaWorld along with Disneyland and Legoland. It was a memorable experience for my children, who loved the excitement and attractions. These visits helped us connect with family in San Diego and Los Angeles as well.
cues: San Diego and Los Angeles visit

summary: Legoland was one of the highlights of our first family trip to the United States. My children were thrilled to explore it along with Disneyland and SeaWorld. This trip also gave us the chance to visit cousins in San Diego and Los Angeles, making it a very special time for us.

On our first visit to the United States, we had the opportunity to visit some cousins who live in San Diego. It was a meaningful part of our trip, allowing my children to connect with family. This visit, along with stops in Los Angeles and theme parks, made the experience unforgettable.

We also visited cousins in Los Angeles during our first trip to the United States. This visit was part of a joyful family experience that included Disneyland, SeaWorld, and Legoland. Seeing family there helped ease the transition as we later moved to the U.S.
